text: Efficient C-C bond cleavage and oxidation of alcohols to CO2 is highly desired for direct alcohol fuel cells (DAFCs) based on renewable energy. Here, we developed a new core/shell Au/Pt nanowires (NWs) catalyst with stepped Pt clusters deposited along the ultrathin (2.3 nm) stepped Au NWs for highly efficient alcohol oxidation to CO2. The Au/Pt ratios influence significantly the catalytic oxidation reaction toward CO2. At Au1.0/Pt0.2 catalyst, about 75% stepped Au/Pt sites were reserved and demonstrated the highest ethanol electro-oxidation activity with the current density of 196.9 A/mgPt (32.5 A/mgPt+Au). In addition, other primary alcohols including methanol, n-propanol and ethylene glycol were also well-oxidized using this unique NW catalyst structure. In-situ X-ray absorption spectroscopy (XAS) and in-situ infrared spectroscopy have been carried out to study the catalyst structure and the intermediates in the oxidation reaction. These experiment results along with density functional theory (DFT) calculation demonstrate that the synergistic effect between low-coordinated Pt sites and stepped Au structure of the ultrathin Au NWs plays a crucial role for efficient alcohol oxidation reaction, and C-C bond cleavage was enhanced on the undercoordinated Pt-Au surface. Therefore, our research confirms that the unique core/shell structure with stepped core/shell sites is promising for efficient electrochemical oxidation of alcohols and provides further fundamental knowledge for other related electro-oxidation reactions, and thus has great potentials for highly efficient direct alcohol fuel cells (DAFCs) to realize renewable energy applications.
